sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.100 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: Suppose that$A$and$G$are finite groups such that$A$acts coprimely on$G$by automorphisms. Let$C = { C}_G(A)$be the fixed-point subgroup, and let$C^$denote its derived subgroup. Is it true that the number of$A$-invariant irreducible characters$$of$G$whose restriction$_C$is never zero is exactly$|C/C'|$? This would follow if one could show that$_C$is never zero if and only if the Glauberman--Isaacs c...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
